meaning: adj.过激的,极端的n.过激论者,急进论者
examples: Not all the glass technology that touches our lives is ultra-modern.
The new ultra-low emission zone (Ulez) is likely to be a big issue in next year's mayoral election.
Our cheap, ultra-reliable wired networks made credit-card authentication over the phone frictionless.
Built into a mountainside, the ultra-modern, Barcelona style is designed to blend into the landscape.
The article 'Mexico City's ultra-niche museums' was published in partnership with Lonely Planet.
